name: Hotel Blowout Feature 1
description: This is a very interesting spring that was formed from a bore hole many years ago. It has a steep embankment on the north west side with Puarenga Stream flowing to it's south. A pump moves water from the spring which looks like the neighbouring hotel uses as a hot water source. This overflows water into the stream. Vegetation overhanging on embankment side. Grey sediment around edges.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

Grey sediment around edges.

name: Hotel Blowout Feature 2
description: Milky blue coloured spring about 8 metres east of Hotel Blowout. A large Manuka tree overhangs it on the south side of spring. It has a couple of very small inputs from 50 degree Celsuis springs. One has a build up of orange biofilm. The spring itself has a small outflow into Feature 3. Yellow coloured sediment.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Hotel Blowout Feature 3
description: Another milky blue pool but with much more floating material and bubbles. A ring of yellow precipitate has built up around the edges. There are some 85 degree Celsuis bubbling black springs on north west side, too small to sample. Input into this feature. No immediate vegetation and spring connects with Feature 4.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

The spring has a catchment area south of it and it outflows into the another catchment stream with Feature 6. Small amount of dead twigs in spring. No other surrounding vegetation.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Te Puia Feature 100
description: Very interesting feature that appears relatively new due to all the dead vegetation around it. It's a geyser that erupts every 20 to 30 seconds and lasts for approximately 5 seconds. Couldn't sample at source so sample taken 2 metres downstream immediately after an eruption. Outflows into Puarenga Stream.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Te Kopia Feature 1
description: This feature is situated on the bottom of the escarpment at Te Kopia. It's completely surrounded by dense bush and much of it overhangs the pool. No visible outputs. The pool is surrounded by orange sediment which gives the water an orangy, light brown colour. Some floating scum on top.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
location_text: Te Kopia Scenic Reserve, Te Kopia, Rotorua
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

The pool is surrounded by orange sediment which gives the water an orangy, light brown colour. Some floating scum on top.

name: Te Kopia Feature 2
description: Awesome collection of shallow hot springs on the extreme south side of the Te Kopia geothermal field. These springs are hidden next to a steep embankment by the steaming cliffs. The springs have a crispy silica crust around the edges. No immediate vegetation except for some dead vegetation above a metre above. All the springs look connected so we only sampled two.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
location_text: Te Kopia Scenic Reserve, Te Kopia, Rotorua
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Awesome collection of shallow hot springs on the extreme south side of the Te Kopia geothermal field. These springs are hidden next to a steep embankment by the steaming cliffs. The springs have a crispy silica crust around the edges. No immediate vegetation except for some dead vegetation above a metre above. All the springs look connected so we only sampled two.

name: Te Kopia Feature 3
description: Another spring in this awesome collection on the south side of Te Kopia. The spring is surrounded by a crisp crust which also has lots of holes with bubbling. There's probably water underneath the entire crust. No immediate vegetation. Sediment a pale cream colour with reddy brown on the crust.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
location_text: Te Kopia Scenic Reserve, Te Kopia, Rotorua
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

There's probably water underneath the entire crust. No immediate vegetation. Sediment a pale cream colour with reddy brown on the crust.

name: Te Kopia Feature 4
description: This little spring is about four metres east of Feature 3. It has a more blue colour than the rest of the springs and it hides out underneath a rock. No visible outputs but it probably flows underneath the crust surrounding it. No immediate vegetation.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Te Kopia Feature 5

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Te Kopia Feature 7
description: This lake sits at the south end of Te Kopia geothermal field, at the bottom of the escarpment. There is huge build up of sediment at the sides, and lots of little bubbling spots in the ground near where the sample was taken. A stream flows from lake but not visible from sampling spot. Surrounded by dense bush on most sides.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

Surrounded by dense bush on most sides.

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Waiotapu Stream Feature 1
description: Waiotapu Stream just before it enters the Waikato River. Sample taken from the grounds of River Lodge. Stream looks turbid. Overhanging vegetation and reeds. Some floating leaves on top.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

name: Waiotapu Stream Feature 2

name: Waiotapu Stream Feature 3
description: Waiotapu Stream taken at Reporoa township next to the river gauge. Lots of overhanging blackberry. Smells like hydrocarbons. Not as many reeds in stream as Feature 2. Farm input.

name: Orakei Korako Feature 12
description: Map of Africa at the top of Rainbow Terrace. Devil's Throat outflows into it. A thick orange spongy biomat surrounds spring. No visible outflows and no vegetation nearby.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

name: Orakei Korako Feature 13
description: Small spring about six metres west of Map of Africa, by the bush line. No visible outputs and surrounded by a hard build up of sinter. Some overhanging vegetation about half a metre back.

name: Orakei Korako Feature 14
description: Hochstetter's Cauldron at Orakei Korako. Magnificent spring surrounded by zero vegetation and a hard sinter. Orange biomass on east side where a small inflow from Map of Africa joins it. The spring outflows down Rainbow Terrace and goes under the boardwalk.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Orakei Korako Feature 15
description: Small spring right at the base of the Golden Fleece. Surrounded by zero vegetation and a hard sinter, this spring upwells intermittently. No visible outflows.

name: Orakei Korako Feature 17
description: Wairiri Geyser. No activity at present. Not outflowing but looks like it used to. One small Manuka shrub overhangs it and there is green biomass on the walls above it.

name: Orakei Korako Feature 18

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Orakei Korako Feature 21
description: Diamond Geyser at Orakei Korako. Spring is currently dormant. Surrounded by a hard black and white sinter. Outflows down an embankment to boardwalk. No immediate vegetation.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Waimangu Stream Feature 1
description: Stream that flows from Frying Pan Lake. Sample taken as close to the start of the outlet as possible. Lots of gas and green biomass floating.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Stream that flows from Frying Pan Lake. Sample taken as close to the start of the outlet as possible. Lots of gas and green biomass floating.

name: Waimangu Stream Feature 2
description: Waimangu Stream, about 150m downstream from lake and where feature 1 was taken. Fast flowing.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Waimangu Stream, about 150m downstream from lake and where feature 1 was taken. Fast flowing.

name: Waimangu Stream Feature 3
description: This sample was taken further downstream of Feature 2, at first bridge that crosses over Waimangu Stream. Lots of green biomass on the bed of the stream. Matt's phone likes to swim here sometimes. Lots of overhanging grass on the stream banks too.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This sample was taken further downstream of Feature 2, at first bridge that crosses over Waimangu Stream. Lots of green biomass on the bed of the stream. Matt's phone likes to swim here sometimes. Lots of overhanging grass on the stream banks too.

name: Waimangu Stream Feature 4
description: This sample was taken from Waimangu Stream, downstream from the Inferno Crater inlet and the second bridge of stream, but before Clamshell Spring. There is a large rock on the stream's edge. Long grass overhangs the banks.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This sample was taken from Waimangu Stream, downstream from the Inferno Crater inlet and the second bridge of stream, but before Clamshell Spring. There is a large rock on the stream's edge. Long grass overhangs the banks.

name: Waimangu Stream Feature 5
description: This sampling point was near Bus Stop 1 at Waimangu, down the embankment by the start of the path to Inferno Crater. It is fast flowing with lots of green biomass. The stream separates into a v-shape by an island immediately after the sampling point. Lots of trees overhang also.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This sampling point was near Bus Stop 1 at Waimangu, down the embankment by the start of the path to Inferno Crater. It is fast flowing with lots of green biomass. The stream separates into a v-shape by an island immediately after the sampling point. Lots of trees overhang also.

name: Waimangu Stream Feature 7
description: Sample taken near inlet of Waimangu Stream to Lake Rotomahana. Lots of black swans nearby at the time of sampling. Some trees overhang also and there is grass on the banks.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Sample taken near inlet of Waimangu Stream to Lake Rotomahana. Lots of black swans nearby at the time of sampling. Some trees overhang also and there is grass on the banks.

name: Waimangu Stream Feature 6
description: This sample was taken close to the bridge near Bus Stop 2 at Waimangu. The stream has a medium flow rate at this point and has also widened considerably from upstream. There is long grass on both banks. And lots of biomass on the bed of the stream.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This sample was taken close to the bridge near Bus Stop 2 at Waimangu. The stream has a medium flow rate at this point and has also widened considerably from upstream. There is long grass on both banks. And lots of biomass on the bed of the stream.

name: Waterfall Spring
description: This spring flows into a small pool (like a small waterfall) which then overflows into the stream at Warbrick Terrace. Lots of vegetation overhangs and rocks make up the spring bed.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This spring flows into a small pool (like a small waterfall) which then overflows into the stream at Warbrick Terrace. Lots of vegetation overhangs and rocks make up the spring bed.

name: Warbrick Terrace Feature 1
description: This is the main spring at Warbrick Terrace. It has a small amount of overhanging vegetation at the back but otherwise, it is surrounded by an orange, spongy biomat. No outflow visible.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This is the main spring at Warbrick Terrace. It has a small amount of overhanging vegetation at the back but otherwise, it is surrounded by an orange, spongy biomat. No outflow visible.

name: Warbrick Terrace Feature 2
description: Small spring, burnt orange in colour, about 30m downstream of Feature 1 on the Warbrick plateau. A green/orange biomat surrounds this spring, small outflow onto the mat.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Small spring, burnt orange in colour, about 30m downstream of Feature 1 on the Warbrick plateau. A green/orange biomat surrounds this spring, small outflow onto the mat.

name: Iodine Pool
description: Iodine Pool at Waimangu is fed from a geyser that erupts regularly. It has a light brown colour, like iodine. No outflow visible and some vegetation overhangs pool.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Iodine Pool at Waimangu is fed from a geyser that erupts regularly. It has a light brown colour, like iodine. No outflow visible and some vegetation overhangs pool.

name: Inferno Crater
description: Larger crater lake with geyser underneath. It has monthly (approx) cycles where the temperature ranges from 30 to 80 degree celsius. At its highest temperature, the lake overflows into a stream that joins with Waimangu Stream near Bird's Nest Geyser.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Larger crater lake with geyser underneath. It has monthly (approx) cycles where the temperature ranges from 30 to 80 degree celsius. At its highest temperature, the lake overflows into a stream that joins with Waimangu Stream near Bird's Nest Geyser.

description: A small spring that's very bubbly and audible. It is adjacent to Waimangu Stream and can be found at feature number 16, just before the first bridge. It outflows into a pool in front of it. Some biofilm overhangs it, otherwise no vegetation.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

description: Pool immediately adjacent to Feature 1 (Feature 1 runs into this pool). However, the pool has individual, small inputs on its bed. Lots of pock-marked grey and brown sediment on bed also. Runs into Waimangu Stream. Vegetation about 30 cm back from the edge.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

marker_description: Small spring next to the board walk at Waiotapu, which sits to the left of Jean Batten Geyser. There are no visible outputs or vegetation surrounding this spring.

name: Devil's Bath
description: This spring has a vivid green colour. Some sparse vegetation hangs out around the edges but most of this is dead. There is a build up of yellow precipitation at the edge of the water. A stream flows into the spring.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This spring has a vivid green colour. Some sparse vegetation hangs out around the edges but most of this is dead. There is a build up of yellow precipitation at the edge of the water. A stream flows into the spring.

name: Champagne Pool
description: Most iconic hotspring in New Zealand. Amorphous red and orange precipitate around the edges. Low volume outflow over a sinter terrace. No vegetation near edges. Lots of steam clouds.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Most iconic hotspring in New Zealand. Amorphous red and orange precipitate around the edges. Low volume outflow over a sinter terrace. No vegetation near edges. Lots of steam clouds.

name: Waiotapu No.13 Feature 1
description: Very fast flowing stream, sample taken slightly upstream from the geyser side. Some vegetation overhangs this feature.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Very fast flowing stream, sample taken slightly upstream from the geyser side. Some vegetation overhangs this feature.

name: Waiotapu No.13 Feature 2
description: Small spring that used to geyser but has been dormant for awhile. No vegetation surrounds it and there are no visible outputs. There is a hard sinter around this spring.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Small spring that used to geyser but has been dormant for awhile. No vegetation surrounds it and there are no visible outputs. There is a hard sinter around this spring.

name: Waiotapu No.13 Feature 3

attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: Furthest spring to the right side of the bridge. It slighty overflows into the stream and some dead vegetation hangs out at one side.

name: Waiotapu No.13 Feature 4

marker_description: Small spring with lots of precipitate on the bottom and around the edges. It slightly overflows into the output from Feature 3, and then into the stream. No vegetation except for a couple of dead branches around the edges.

name: Waiotapu No.13 Feature 5
description: This spring is adjacent to Feature 4 (to the left if looking from the bridge), but has more bubbling and gas discharge, and looks more turbid. Some very dead twigs have fallen into the spring, and there is a small outflow into the nearby stream.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

marker_description: This spring is adjacent to Feature 4 (to the left if looking from the bridge), but has more bubbling and gas discharge, and looks more turbid. Some very dead twigs have fallen into the spring, and there is a small outflow into the nearby stream.

name: Waiotapu No.13 Feature 6
description: This is a very slow flowing stream, that has a build up of yellow precipitate on its bed (most likely due to arsenic). The spring has a large overflow into the stream. No vegetation around it except for some dead twigs.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This is a very slow flowing stream, that has a build up of yellow precipitate on its bed (most likely due to arsenic). The spring has a large overflow into the stream. No vegetation around it except for some dead twigs.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 1
description: This spring is bubbling vigorously and looks very shallow. No vegetation exists nearby and the spring has some very slight overflow (perhaps more if the water table is higher).
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This spring is bubbling vigorously and looks very shallow. No vegetation exists nearby and the spring has some very slight overflow (perhaps more if the water table is higher).

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 2
description: This sample was taken from the converging point of three streams at the Alum Cliffs (directly across from the No.14 sign). The stream is very fast flowing at this point and there is little vegetation nearby.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 3

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 4
description: Small spring across the stream in front of the Alum Cliffs. This spring has lots of dead branches overhanging it and there a very small outflow into the stream. There is brown sediment on the bottom and a steep embankment to one side.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

marker_description: Small spring across the stream in front of the Alum Cliffs. This spring has lots of dead branches overhanging it and there a very small outflow into the stream. There is brown sediment on the bottom and a steep embankment to one side.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 5
description: This is the first spring in a series of five on the banks where the stream flows into the lake at the Alum Cliffs. This one was sampled by Katrin Hug and is immediately adjacent to the path. Zero vegetation surrounds it and there is some slight outflow into the stream.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

marker_description: This is the first spring in a series of five on the banks where the stream flows into the lake at the Alum Cliffs. This one was sampled by Katrin Hug and is immediately adjacent to the path. Zero vegetation surrounds it and there is some slight outflow into the stream.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 6
description: This is the second spring in this series of five. It is small and immediately adjacent to the stream. There is no vegetation nearby, except for a dead branch near one end. The spring overflows into the stream.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

marker_description: This is the second spring in this series of five. It is small and immediately adjacent to the stream. There is no vegetation nearby, except for a dead branch near one end. The spring overflows into the stream.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 8
description: Katrin's spring, the fourth in this series near the Alum Cliffs. This spring is a vivid yellow colour due to high levels of arsenic. It is surrounded by small rocks and outflows into the lake. No vegetation.

marker_description: Katrin's spring, the fourth in this series near the Alum Cliffs. This spring is a vivid yellow colour due to high levels of arsenic. It is surrounded by small rocks and outflows into the lake. No vegetation.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 9
description: Very small spring just downstream of Feature 8. This one is more green in colour than yellow. No vegetation nearby and it outflows into the lake. Feature 8 flows into this spring sometimes.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 10
description: This sample was taken from the large spring/lake by the Alum Cliffs. The stream flows into this lake, and then it outflows into Frying Pan Flat. The sample was taken right by the path after the Alum Cliffs sign, on the left side of the lake. Some spare vegetation, half dead, hangs out on the edges of this lake. There is a green tinge to the colour.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 11
description: This spring is situated across the stream from the path at the Alum Cliffs. There is no visible outflow, but possibly one occurs if the water table is higher. No vegetation surrounds this spring and it looks murky and slightly green.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Alum Cliffs Feature 12
description: This spring is also across the stream at the Alum Cliffs like Feature 11, but this one is closer to the embankment. It's also ten degrees hotter and less turbid looking. No vegetation exists near this spring, except for a few dead branches. There is no visible output from this spring, and it is surrounded by geothermal-altered ground.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Prawn Park Feature 1
description: These set of terraces have been developed by Andrew at the Prawn Park. Silica has deposited out of solution to form silica terraces. This sample was taken from the second terrace after the main inlet. It outflows into Wairakei Stream which then continues to join the mighty Waikato River. There is no vegetation near this terrace.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: These set of terraces have been developed by Andrew at the Prawn Park. Silica has deposited out of solution to form silica terraces. This sample was taken from the second terrace after the main inlet. It outflows into Wairakei Stream which then continues to join the mighty Waikato River. There is no vegetation near this terrace.

name: Prawn Park Feature 2
description: This sample was taken from the first or main terrace that the output pipe flows into to. There is a hole in the terrace at the entry point of the fluid. Some low-lying grass exists on one edge of the terrace, otherwise no vegetation. The terrace outflows into Wairakei Stream, and then onto the mighty Wairakei River.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

name: Prawn Park Feature 3
description: To the right of main pipe, there is a small pot that a pipe flows into. The sample was taken from this (pipe overflow). It has purple precipitate around the edges, and outflows into Feature 2. Zero vegetation nearby.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

name: Waimangu No.13 Feature 1
description: This tiny spring sits on the banks of Waimangu Stream, in between Frying Pan Lake and Inferno Crater. There is another small spring immediately adjacent to it. The spring has green and orange biomass growing around it and it has a small outflow. No vegetation overhanging.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: This tiny spring sits on the banks of Waimangu Stream, in between Frying Pan Lake and Inferno Crater. There is another small spring immediately adjacent to it. The spring has green and orange biomass growing around it and it has a small outflow. No vegetation overhanging.

name: Waimangu No.13 Feature 2
description: This spring lies immediately to the right of Feature 1 on the banks of Waimangu Stream, and the two are only separated by approx 5 cm. This one is also small, and has the same green and orange biomass growing around it. There is some slight mixing between the two springs, yet there is over 20 C difference between their temperatures.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.

No immediate vegetation, except for a small dead branch on one side and there is grey sediment visible on the bottom. Bubbles float on top of this spring. We had cake to celebrate our 100th sample! name: Wairakei Terraces Feature 16

attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

name: Wairakei Terraces Bathing Pool 1
description: The first of the bathing pools after the main terraces (and therefore the hottest). Vivid blue in colour due to the silica content. The source of the four bathing pools is the main geyser on top of the terraces so they are all linked. A small amount of vegetation overhangs this pool.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
marker_description: The first of the bathing pools after the main terraces (and therefore the hottest). Vivid blue in colour due to the silica content. The source of the four bathing pools is the main geyser on top of the terraces so they are all linked. A small amount of vegetation overhangs this pool.

name: Wairakei Terraces Bathing Pool 2

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

name: Wairakei Terraces Bathing Pool 4

safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

name: Te Puia Feature 51
description: Very interesting spring that has a major upwelling intermittenly, after 5 minutes or 20 minutes. The upwelling lasts 2-3 minutes. Zero vegetation surrounding and no visible inputs or outputs. A steam fumerole exists on the south side which vents steam and liquid in between eruptions of the main spring.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
location_text: Te Puia (Te Whakarewarewa Valley), Rotorua, Rotorua
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

name: Lake Rotomahana Feature 1

name: Lake Rotomahana Feature 2
description: Another feature of the Steaming Cliffs known as Williamson Spring. It sits a couple of metres about the water level where hot water flows into the lake, left of the Angel Wings. Yellow biofilm growing around the extreme edges. The sample was taken just underneath geyser before it entered the lake.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato

Yellow biofilm growing around the extreme edges. The sample was taken just underneath geyser before it entered the lake.

name: Lake Rotomahana Feature 3
description: This geyser is called Old Blackie and it's also part of the Steaming Cliffs. It erupts every four minutes so the sample was taken in between eruptions. It sits as a hot pool with a sinter surrounding it (like a cave) and then starts flowing into the lake just before eruptions. No surrounding vegetation.
safety_warning: Hot springs can cause serious injury and death. Follow the safety instructions. Never climb over barriers and step off paths.
attribution: 1000 Springs Project authors, GNS Science, and University of Waikato
